reasoning: Governor Greg Abbott vetoed Senate Bill 3 in late June 2025, which was the primary legislation intended to fully ban the sale of THC in Texas. There were no other bills enacting a full ban on THC signed into law before the July 1, 2025 deadline. The legislature held special sessions later in the summer to discuss the issue further, and Abbott issued an executive order regarding hemp regulation in September 2025, but a full ban was not enacted into law prior to July 1, 2025 as required by the resolution criteria. Partial bans, such as those on vapes passed during the session, explicitly do not count. The evidence strongly supports a 'No' resolution.
